sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 25-year-old female, presents for evaluation of breast reduction.
She has been experiencing symptoms of macromastia, including back pain, neck pain, and bra strap indentations, for approximately 5-7 years.
She has been considering breast reduction surgery for about 6-7 years and has attempted nonsurgical treatments with minimal relief.
She reports that her activities are limited due to the size of her breasts, particularly when performing exercises like working out, jumping, or running.
She has a history of chiropractic care for back pain but does not take pain medication.
She is currently a student studying psychology and consumes occasional wine.
She denies tobacco, drug, or alcohol use.
She is not interested in breastfeeding.
Past medical history: She has a family history of breast cancer, with her mother diagnosed at age 40.
She has had mammograms recommended to start at age 30.
She has been healthy for the most part, having lost 10 pounds in the past few months and aiming to lose 20 more pounds.
Review of systems: She experiences occasional numbness or tingling in her hands and sometimes has nipple pain.
She does not have rashes underneath her breasts.
Current medications: Propranolol for migraines.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]

Physical exam: Physical exam findings reveal asymmetrical breasts with severe ptosis bilaterally, no nipple retraction or discharge, no breast tenderness, masses, or axillary lymphadenopathy is palpable.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is an ideal candidate for bilateral reduction mammoplasty due to symptomatic macromastia and minimal relief from nonsurgical treatments.
Problem list: Macromastia; Back pain; Neck pain; Bra strap indentations; Nipple pain; Occasional numbness or tingling in hands.
Plan:
I prescribed Propranolol as needed for migraines.
I will document the patient's symptoms and measurements, which will be sent to the insurance company for approval.
Once the insurance company approves, we will schedule the patient for surgery.
The surgery will involve removing excess tissue and lifting the breasts to alleviate back and neck pain.
The patient will have drains in place for about a week, and the incision will be covered with clear plastic tape to aid in healing.
I will keep the patient overnight in the hospital for pain management and nausea control.
The patient will have activity restrictions for 6 weeks post-surgery, including no heavy lifting and no driving for 2-3 weeks.
Follow-up: The patient will meet with the office again before surgery to review final instructions and answer any last-minute questions.
